objective: Distinguish between perimeter (distance around) and area (space inside) and calculate both for rectangles

pretest.question: What is the difference between perimeter and area?
choices: A) Perimeter is the space inside; area is the distance around
B) Perimeter is the distance around; area is the space inside
C) They are the same thing
hints: Perimeter is the distance ALL the way around. Add every side.

feedback.correct: Nice work! The answer is B (Perimeter is the distance around; area is the space inside).
feedback.incorrect: The answer is B (Perimeter is the distance around; area is the space inside). Perimeter is the distance ALL the way around. Add every side.

pretest.question: A rectangle is 5 cm long and 3 cm wide. What is the area (in square)?
hints: Area is rows × columns (in square units).

feedback.correct: Exactly! The answer is 15.
feedback.incorrect: The answer is 15. Area is rows × columns (in square units).

pretest.question: A rectangle is 6 m long and 4 m wide. What is the perimeter?
hints: Perimeter is the distance ALL the way around. Add every side.

feedback.correct: Good job! The answer is A (20 m).
feedback.incorrect: The answer is A (20 m). Perimeter is the distance ALL the way around. Add every side.

pretest.question: Which unit measures area?
hints: Area is rows × columns (in square units).

feedback.correct: Right! The answer is B (square centimeters).
feedback.incorrect: The answer is B (square centimeters). Area is rows × columns (in square units).

pretest.question: A square has a side of 4 cm. What is the area?
hints: Area is rows × columns (in square units).

feedback.correct: Yes! The answer is 16.
feedback.incorrect: The answer is 16. Area is rows × columns (in square units).

learn.instruction: PERIMETER = distance AROUND a shape. Measured in linear units (cm, m, ft). AREA = space INSIDE a shape. Measured in square units (sq cm, sq m, sq ft). For a rectangle: P = (l + w) × 2. Area = l × w. Example: Rectangle 7 cm × 3 cm. P = (7+3)×2 = 20 cm. Area = 7 × 3 = 21 sq cm.
learn.question: A rectangle is 8 cm long and 2 cm wide. What is the perimeter AND the area?
learn.content: PERIMETER = distance AROUND a shape. Measured in linear units (cm, m, ft). AREA = space INSIDE a shape. Measured in square units (sq cm, sq m, sq ft). For a rectangle: P = (l + w) × 2. Area = l × w. Example: Rectangle 7 cm × 3 cm. P = (7+3)×2 = 20 cm. Area = 7 × 3 = 21 sq cm.

learn.instruction: Two shapes can have the SAME perimeter but DIFFERENT areas. Rectangle A: 6×2 → P=16, Area=12. Rectangle B: 5×3 → P=16, Area=15. Same perimeter (16), different area!
learn.question: Rectangle A is 7 cm × 1 cm. Rectangle B is 4 cm × 4 cm. Do they have the same perimeter? Which has greater area?
learn.answer: Both have perimeter 16 cm. Rectangle B has greater area (16 sq cm vs 7 sq cm).
learn.explanation: A: P=(7+1)×2=16, Area=7. B: P=(4+4)×2=16, Area=16. Same P, B has greater area.
learn.content: Two shapes can have the SAME perimeter but DIFFERENT areas. Rectangle A: 6×2 → P=16, Area=12. Rectangle B: 5×3 → P=16, Area=15. Same perimeter (16), different area!

learn.instruction: Two shapes can have the SAME area but DIFFERENT perimeters. Rectangle A: 8×2 → Area=16, P=20. Rectangle B: 4×4 → Area=16, P=16. Same area (16 sq units), different perimeters!
learn.question: Rectangle A is 9 cm × 1 cm. Rectangle B is 3 cm × 3 cm. Do they have the same area? Which has greater perimeter?
learn.answer: Both have area 9 sq cm. Rectangle A has greater perimeter (20 cm vs 12 cm).
learn.explanation: A: Area=9×1=9, P=(9+1)×2=20. B: Area=3×3=9, P=(3+3)×2=12. Same area, A has greater perimeter.
learn.content: Two shapes can have the SAME area but DIFFERENT perimeters. Rectangle A: 8×2 → Area=16, P=20. Rectangle B: 4×4 → Area=16, P=16. Same area (16 sq units), different perimeters!

try.question: A rectangle is 9 m long and 4 m wide. Perimeter = __ m. Area = __ sq m.
hints: Use P = (l+w)×2 and Area = l×w.

feedback.incorrect: and 36 sq m
feedback.correct: Correct!

try.question: A square has a side of 5 cm. What is the perimeter and area?
choices: A) P = 20 cm, Area = 25 sq cm
B) P = 25 cm, Area = 20 sq cm
C) P = 20 cm, Area = 20 sq cm
hints: P = 4×side. Area = side×side.

feedback.correct: Yes! P=20 cm, Area=25 sq cm.
feedback.incorrect: Square 5 cm: P = 4 × 5 = 20 cm. Area = 5 × 5 = 25 sq cm.

try.question: Rectangle A: 6 cm × 3 cm. Rectangle B: 9 cm × 2 cm. Which statement is true?
choices: A) They have the same perimeter and same area
B) They have the same perimeter but different areas
C) They have different perimeters and different areas
guided_steps: Wait — recalculate: A P=18, B P=22. Different P, same Area.
hints: Calculate both perimeter and area for each rectangle.

feedback.correct: Right! Both have perimeter 18 cm but different areas.
feedback.incorrect: A: P=18, Area=18. B: P=22, Area=18 — actually A and B differ. Recheck: A perimeter (6+3)×2=18 cm, B perimeter (9+2)×2=22 cm. Same area 18 sq cm but different perimeters.

feedback.correct: Great! The answer is C (They are equal (18 cm)).

practice_r2.question: A room is 12 feet long and 9 feet wide. How much carpet is needed to cover the floor (area)? How much baseboard is needed around the edges (perimeter)?
hints: Perimeter is the distance ALL the way around. Add every side.

feedback.correct: Exactly! The answer is Area = 108 sq ft. Perimeter = (12+9)×2 = 42 ft.
feedback.incorrect: The answer is Area = 108 sq ft. Perimeter = (12+9)×2 = 42 ft. Perimeter is the distance ALL the way around. Add every side.

practice_r2.question: A garden is 8 m × 5 m. The gardener wants to fence it (perimeter) and plant grass (area). What are the perimeter and area?
choices: A) P = 26 m, Area = 40 sq m
B) P = 40 m, Area = 26 sq m
C) P = 26 m, Area = 13 sq m
hints: Multiplication is repeated addition. Use known facts when possible.

feedback.correct: Good job! The answer is A (P = 26 m, Area = 40 sq m).
feedback.incorrect: The answer is A (P = 26 m, Area = 40 sq m). Multiplication is repeated addition. Use known facts when possible.

practice_r2.question: Two playgrounds both have an area of 24 sq m. Playground A is 8 m × 3 m. Playground B is 6 m × 4 m. Which needs more fencing?
practice_r2.correct_answer: Playground A needs more fencing. A: P=(8+3)×2=22 m. B: P=(6+4)×2=20 m.
hints: Multiplication is repeated addition. Use known facts when possible.

feedback.correct: Right! The answer is Playground A needs more fencing. A: P=(8+3)×2=22 m. B: P=(6+4)×2=20 m.
feedback.incorrect: The answer is Playground A needs more fencing. A: P=(8+3)×2=22 m. B: P=(6+4)×2=20 m. Multiplication is repeated addition. Use known facts when possible.

practice_r3.question: Find two different rectangles that each have an area of 36 sq cm. Calculate the perimeter of each. Which has the smaller perimeter?
practice_r3.correct_answer: Example: 9×4 → Area=36, P=26. 6×6 → Area=36, P=24. The square (6×6) has the smaller perimeter.
hints: Perimeter is the distance ALL the way around. Add every side.

feedback.correct: Correct! The answer is Example: 9×4 → Area=36, P=26. 6×6 → Area=36, P=24. The square (6×6) has the smaller perimeter.
feedback.incorrect: The answer is Example: 9×4 → Area=36, P=26. 6×6 → Area=36, P=24. The square (6×6) has the smaller perimeter. Perimeter is the distance ALL the way around. Add every side.

practice_r3.question: Explain in your own words why perimeter and area are different. Why can two shapes have the same perimeter but different areas?
practice_r3.correct_answer: Perimeter measures distance around (linear). Area measures space inside (square units). Shapes can be stretched differently — a long thin rectangle and a squarish rectangle can have the same perimeter but different amounts of space inside.
hints: Perimeter is the distance ALL the way around. Add every side.

feedback.correct: Great! The answer is Perimeter measures distance around (linear). Area measures space inside (square units). Shapes can be stretched differently — a long thin rectangle and a squarish rectangle can have the same perimeter but different amounts of space inside.
feedback.incorrect: The answer is Perimeter measures distance around (linear). Area measures space inside (square units). Shapes can be stretched differently — a long thin rectangle and a squarish rectangle can have the same perimeter but different amounts of space inside. Perimeter is the distance ALL the way around. Add every side.

practice_r3.question: A rectangle has an area of 20 sq cm and a perimeter of 18 cm. What are the length and width?
practice_r3.correct_answer: Length = 5 cm, Width = 4 cm. Check: Area = 5×4=20 ✓. P=(5+4)×2=18 ✓.
hints: Perimeter is the distance ALL the way around. Add every side.

feedback.correct: Nice work! The answer is Length = 5 cm, Width = 4 cm. Check: Area = 5×4=20 ✓. P=(5+4)×2=18 ✓.
feedback.incorrect: The answer is Length = 5 cm, Width = 4 cm. Check: Area = 5×4=20 ✓. P=(5+4)×2=18 ✓. Perimeter is the distance ALL the way around. Add every side.

title: Understand Perimeter and Area
